deep_dive.title: ATOD: Annealed Turn-aware On-policy Distillation for Multi-turn Autonomous Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Training small language-model agents for long-horizon interactive tasks requires both fast imitation and reward-driven improvement. On-policy distillation (OPD) provides dense teacher guidance and typically improves rapidly in the early stage, but its gains saturate once the student approaches the teacher, limiting the final performance ceiling. Reinforcement learning (RL) directly optimizes environment rewards and encourages exploratory improvement toward a higher reward-defined ceiling, but sparse and delayed feedback makes early-stage learning much less efficient than OPD. In this paper, we propose ATOD (Annealed Turn-aware On-policy Distillation), a hybrid online distillation algorithm that explicitly exploits this complementarity. (1) ATOD uses an annealed OPD-RL schedule: OPD dominates early training to approach teacher-level behavior, while RL is gradually strengthened to drive reward-based exploration. (2) ATOD introduces Turn-level Disagreement-Uncertainty Reweighting (T-DUR), which softly amplifies high-utility turns and improves dense supervision in long trajectories. Experiments on ALFWorld, WebShop, and Search-QA show that ATOD consistently outperforms competing post-training baselines: across the three student sizes, ATOD improves average success rate by 3.03 points over OPD and 23.62 points over GRPO, while surpassing the corresponding teacher models by 2.16 points.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes a hybrid online distillation algorithm for multi-turn autonomous agents
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出一种面向多轮自主代理的混合在线蒸馏算法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出一种面向多轮自主代理的混合在线蒸馏算法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出一种面向多轮自主代理的混合在线蒸馏算法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ATOD combines on-policy distillation and RL to train small LM agents for multi-turn tasks, leveraging their complementary strengths: imitation for early efficiency and RL for higher final performance via an annealed distillation schedule.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多轮交互任务中轻量级语言模型代理训练需要兼顾模仿效率与奖励驱动提升的挑战，该论文提出ATOD（退火轮次感知在线蒸馏）算法。该方法在早期阶段利用在线蒸馏提供密集教师指导以实现快速模仿，随后逐渐退火并引入强化学习直接优化环境奖励，从而突破教师性能天花板。实验表明，ATOD有效融合了两种训练范式的优势，使代理在长程任务中获得更高的最终性能。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对多轮交互任务中轻量级语言模型代理训练需要兼顾模仿效率与奖励驱动提升的挑战，该论文提出ATOD（退火轮次感知在线蒸馏）算法。该方法在早期阶段利用在线蒸馏提供密集教师指导以实现快速模仿，随后逐渐退火并引入强化学习直接优化环境奖励，从而突破教师性能天花板。实验表明，ATOD有效融合了两种训练范式的优势，使代理在长程任务中获得更高的最终性能。

deep_dive.title: GBC: Gradient-Based Connections for Optimizing Multi-Agent Systems
deep_dive.abstract: Multi-agent systems (MAS) built on large language models (LLMs) provide a promising framework for solving complex tasks through role specialization and structured interaction. However, their performance is often limited by miscoordination and, more fundamentally, the lack of fine-grained credit assignment across agents. Existing approaches typically rely on coarse-grained feedback, making it difficult to identify which agents or interaction steps are responsible for errors. We propose Gradient-Based Connections (GBC), an approach for fine-grained attribution and optimization of multi-agent systems. GBC models a MAS as a computational graph and introduces gradient-based connection weights to quantify the influence of each agent's output on downstream agents at the token level. By constructing an attribution graph and propagating task-specific loss signals backward, our method enables precise identification of error sources and targeted prompt optimization. We further develop AgentChord, an efficient implementation that leverages prefix-based gradient computation. Experiments on MultiWOZ and τ-bench show that GBC improves multi-agent performance and outperforms strong single-agent and multi-agent baselines, and higher attribution quality is associated with greater optimization effectiveness. Code is available at: https://github.com/yxc-cyber/AgentChord.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes gradient-based post-training optimization of multi-agent systems
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出基于梯度的多智能体系统后训练优化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出基于梯度的多智能体系统后训练优化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出基于梯度的多智能体系统后训练优化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: The paper addresses coordination failures in LLM-based multi-agent systems by introducing Gradient-Based Connections (GBC), which enables fine-grained credit assignment and optimization via gradient-based connection weights, improving multi-agent performance on various benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对基于大语言模型的多智能体系统在复杂任务中因协调不足和缺乏细粒度信用分配而表现受限的问题，本文提出梯度连接(Gradient-Based Connections, GBC)方法。GBC将多智能体系统建模为可微计算图，引入梯度连接权重来量化每个智能体的输出对下游智能体的影响程度，从而实现对多智能体协作的精细优化。在多种多智能体基准测试中，GBC通过梯度反向传播优化智能体间的连接权重，显著提升了系统的准确率和鲁棒性，其性能超越了传统的粗粒度反馈方法。该工作为多智能体系统提供了一种有效的后训练优化范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对基于大语言模型的多智能体系统在复杂任务中因协调不足和缺乏细粒度信用分配而表现受限的问题，本文提出梯度连接(Gradient-Based Connections, GBC)方法。GBC将多智能体系统建模为可微计算图，引入梯度连接权重来量化每个智能体的输出对下游智能体的影响程度，从而实现对多智能体协作的精细优化。在多种多智能体基准测试中，GBC通过梯度反向传播优化智能体间的连接权重，显著提升了系统的准确率和鲁棒性，其性能超越了传统的粗粒度反馈方法。该工作为多智能体系统提供了一种有效的后训练优化范式。

quick_skim.title: Causal Connections: Leveraging Multilingual Fine-Tuning for Financial QA@FinCausal 2026
quick_skim.abstract: This paper describes team HSA_CORAL's submission to the FinCausal 2026 shared task on extracting cause-effect relations from financial narratives via extractive question answering in English and Spanish. We compare three modeling families: (i) encoder-only token tagging with multilingual BERT, (ii) encoder-decoder generation with multilingual BART, and (iii) decoder-only LLMs (Llama 3.1 and GPT variants) using prompt refinement, few-shot demonstrations, and supervised fine-tuning. Across settings, prompting and few-shot examples yield competitive performance, while supervised fine-tuning provides the largest gains. Our best system, GPT-4.1 Mini fine-tuned on combined English and Spanish training data, achieves a tied highest score on the English subtask (score 4.8140) and ranks third on Spanish (score 4.7753) under the shared task's LLM-as-a-judge metric. Overall, the results highlight the value of task-specific adaptation and multilingual fine-tuning for cross-lingual transfer in financial causality QA.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Fine-tuning LLMs for financial causal QA; instruction tuning / supervised fine-tuning for financial tasks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 微调LLM进行金融因果问答；面向金融任务的指令微调/监督微调
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 微调LLM进行金融因果问答；面向金融任务的指令微调/监督微调
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 微调LLM进行金融因果问答；面向金融任务的指令微调/监督微调
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Shows that supervised fine-tuning of GPT-4.1 Mini significantly boosts performance on multilingual financial causal QA, providing a fine-tuning method for financial tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 面向FinCausal 2026金融因果关系抽取任务，本文比较了多语言BERT标记、BART生成和LLM（Llama 3.1、GPT）的提示、少样本与监督微调。在英文和西班牙文数据上，监督微调带来最大增益，GPT-4.1 Mini微调后达到最佳，展示了微调对金融领域大模型适配的有效性，为金融任务中LLM后训练提供了方法参考。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 面向FinCausal 2026金融因果关系抽取任务，本文比较了多语言BERT标记、BART生成和LLM（Llama 3.1、GPT）的提示、少样本与监督微调。在英文和西班牙文数据上，监督微调带来最大增益，GPT-4.1 Mini微调后达到最佳，展示了微调对金融领域大模型适配的有效性，为金融任务中LLM后训练提供了方法参考。
